description: Extension of electricity connections to 1.2 million households in rural and peri-urban areas across Kenya's 47 counties, targeting universal electricity access by 2030.
risks: Vandalism of infrastructure. Customer affordability. Contractor capacity.
notes: 624,000 connections completed. World Bank satisfied with implementation progress. Phase 3 on track for completion by 2026.

projectName: Port of Mombasa Berth 22 and 23 Expansion
description: Construction of two new deep-water berths at the Port of Mombasa to increase container handling capacity from 1.6 million to 2.5 million TEUs per annum, including new container yard and equipment.
risks: Marine works contractor delays. Equipment delivery lead times. Environmental compliance.
notes: Berth 22 civil works 80% complete. Berth 23 civil works 50% complete. Quay cranes on order from ZPMC China.

description: Development of 3 additional berths at Lamu Port as part of the LAPSSET Corridor Programme, targeting coal, bulk cargo, and container traffic from Ethiopia and South Sudan.
risks: Security situation in northern Kenya. LAPSSET corridor infrastructure readiness. Environmental impact.
notes: Feasibility study completed. Environmental Impact Assessment in progress. First berth construction expected to commence 2026.

projectName: iTax Phase 3 — Integrated Tax Administration System
description: Upgrade of KRA's iTax platform to include AI-powered risk profiling, real-time VAT monitoring, digital stamp duty, and integration with county government revenue systems.
risks: Vendor delivery delays. Data migration complexity. Taxpayer adoption.
notes: Vendor contract awarded. System design phase completed. Development phase commenced January 2025.

description: NSSF investment of KES 15 billion in affordable housing units across Nairobi, Mombasa, Kisumu, and Nakuru, aligned with the government's Affordable Housing Programme. Target of 10,000 units.
risks: Construction cost inflation. Land acquisition delays. Offtake risk.
notes: 2,800 units under construction. 500 units completed and allocated to NSSF members. Partnership with National Housing Corporation for project management.
